message:
fix: integer precision in columnar ops, N-way concat alignment, and merge_asof direction

- fp-columnar: route Int64 scalar comparisons and arithmetic through native
  i64 operations instead of casting to f64, which silently corrupted values
  beyond 2^53; coerce mismatched numeric types (e.g. Bool vs Int64) to a
  common dtype before comparison; arithmetic on Int64 operands now uses
  wrapping_add/sub/mul directly and returns Int64 without the lossy
  f64-roundtrip-then-trunc path
- fp-frame: replace unwrap()-based limit checks in forward_fill and
  back_fill with map_or for cleaner option handling; add N-way leapfrog
  merge fast-path in DataFrame::concat that calls multi_way_align when
  no duplicate index entries are present, avoiding the O(n*k) pairwise
  align_union cascade for the common case
- fp-join: fix merge_asof backward direction by using partition_point
  with <= instead of <, so that exact matches are correctly included in
  the "backward" window rather than being skipped to the next-smaller value

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
